title: Dashboard Lighthouse Performance

unused_javascript.detail: ~1.95s of script execution time is unused; vendor bundles could be code-split

findings: Performance score 59/100 — primarily due to 18.4s LCP caused by loading 2.26MB of prediction data on initial page load
CLS is 0.0 — no layout shift issues, excellent
TBT is 70ms — minimal main thread blocking
The single biggest performance bottleneck is the /api/predictions call returning 2.26MB JSON on first paint, blocking LCP/TTI/Speed Index
Unused JS (1.95s wasted) — vendor libraries (Recharts, React) are shipped as full bundles without code splitting
Server response time (32ms) and RTT (0.7ms) are excellent — the bottleneck is data transfer and parsing, not network
recommendations: Implement pagination or lazy loading for the predictions list — don't load 2.26MB of JSON on initial page load
Add server-side pagination to /api/predictions endpoint (currently returns ALL predictions)
Code-split vendor bundles (Recharts is only needed on chart pages, not landing)
Consider React.lazy() + Suspense for route-based code splitting
Enable gzip/brotli compression on the dashboard nginx server for JS/CSS assets
